_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, representing an average of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices indexed to 2015 as 100, currently stands at 141, reflecting a 41% increase since 2015. This aggregate measure indicates a significant rise in soft commodity prices over the period. However, this increase has not been steady or uniform across all components or years. The rise is recent and uneven, driven by substantial changes in selected items rather than broad market shifts.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have nearly doubled since 2015, up 99%, making it the largest contributor to the basket’s increase. Orange juice prices are up 34%, followed by wheat at 19% and sugar at 13%. These are markets highly sensitive to weather events and crop diseases—such as citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice, and frost or drought affecting coffee growing regions. Importantly, these soft commodity markets are relatively thin, meaning that changes in crop conditions can cause sharp and volatile price movements rather than reflecting general inflation.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is largely driven by a few crops vulnerable to weather and disease rather than widespread inflationary pressures. Because these commodities face recurring environmental risks, their prices may remain elevated or volatile for extended periods. Understanding these dynamics is key to interpreting movements in soft commodity prices.
